comment: Test prompts seeded for the next-session eval loop. Files reference the three benchmark PDFs in the project folder; copy them into eval input dirs when running. Assertions to be drafted during the run per skill-creator workflow.

evals.prompt: Hey, our department publishes this PDF guide on our website and someone filed a complaint that it doesn't work with their screen reader. Can you check it and tell me what's wrong and how to fix it? I made it in Word originally and still have the .docx somewhere.
evals.expected_output: Runs checker (not text-only guessing); identifies the specific defects with WCAG references; produces plain-language report; classifies tier 1; gives Word-specific fix instructions (title, alt text, header row, tagged export) rather than PDF surgery.

evals.prompt: I have a folder of PDFs we publish and I need to know which ones are accessibility problems and how bad each one is. Can you rank them and tell me what it would take to fix each?
evals.expected_output: Runs checker on all files; ranks by severity (untagged/broken > isolated defects); assigns triage tiers; asks about source file availability; distinguishes author-fixable from project-scale work; does not trust 'Tagged: yes' alone.

evals.prompt: This old PDF book is important content for us but the source files are long gone. Is it fixable? If you can actually repair it, go ahead — but I need to be sure you don't change how it looks.
evals.expected_output: Audits first; classifies tier 3; works on a copy; applies mechanical fixes (lang/title/bookmarks) and heading re-leveling per remediation patterns; runs pixel-identity verification and reports it; re-runs checker with before/after delta; states honest residuals; does NOT add PDF/UA identifier.
